name: Thermal Conductivity Calculator
description: Industrial-grade thermal conductivity analysis tool for materials, insulation, and heat transfer systems. Compliant with ISO 8301, ASTM C518, and Lean Six Sigma DMAIC principles.

inputs.label: Material Type
inputs.businessContext: Select the material category to apply appropriate thermal conductivity ranges and correction factors per ISO 8301.

inputs.label: Operating Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Mean temperature of the material during operation. Affects phonon scattering and electron transport in solids.

inputs.label: Thickness
inputs.businessContext: Material thickness in the direction of heat flow. Used for thermal resistance calculation.

inputs.label: Cross-Sectional Area
inputs.businessContext: Area perpendicular to heat flow. Critical for heat flux density calculations.

inputs.label: Heat Flow Rate
inputs.businessContext: Steady-state heat transfer rate through the material. Measured via calorimeter or heat flux sensor.

inputs.label: Temperature Difference
inputs.businessContext: Temperature gradient across the material thickness. Drives conductive heat transfer.

inputs.label: Moisture Content
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of moisture by volume. Increases effective thermal conductivity in porous materials (WERC guidelines).

inputs.label: Aging Factor
inputs.businessContext: Multiplier accounting for material degradation over time. Based on Six Sigma aging studies.

inputs.label: Measurement Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data quality. Affects data confidence adjustment in outputs.

rules.message: Temperature difference exceeds 50% of absolute temperature. Check for phase change or measurement error.
Moisture content above 5% in insulation foam significantly degrades thermal performance per ASTM C518.
Heat flux exceeds 10 kW/m². Verify cooling capacity and material limits.

thresholds.message: Effective thermal conductivity exceeds 400 W/(m·K). Typical for pure metals only. Verify inputs.
Thermal resistance below 0.5 m²·K/W. Insulation may be inadequate for building envelope per ISO 6946.
Thermal efficiency below 60%. Consider material replacement or design review (Lean Six Sigma).

formulas.name: Base Thermal Conductivity
formulas.description: Calculates base conductivity from material type and temperature using empirical correlations.
formulas.formula: material_type === 'insulation_foam' ? 0.04 : material_type === 'metal_alloy' ? 50 * (temperature_k / 300) ** 0.5 : material_type === 'ceramic' ? 3 : material_type === 'composite' ? 0.5 : material_type === 'fluid_gas' ? 0.025 : material_type === 'fluid_liquid' ? 0.6 : 1.0

formulas.name: Moisture Correction Factor
formulas.description: Adjusts conductivity for moisture content using WERC standard porous media model.

formulas.name: Aging Correction Factor
formulas.description: Applies aging factor to account for material degradation over time.

formulas.name: Effective Thermal Conductivity
formulas.description: Combines base conductivity with moisture and aging corrections.

formulas.name: Thermal Resistance (R-value)
formulas.description: Calculates R-value from thickness and effective conductivity per ISO 6946.

formulas.name: Heat Flux Density
formulas.description: Calculates heat flux per unit area from Fourier's law.

formulas.name: Thermal Efficiency
formulas.description: Ratio of actual heat transfer to ideal (no-loss) scenario. Used in Six Sigma process capability analysis.

primary.label: Effective Thermal Conductivity
primary.description: Primary result: material's effective thermal conductivity after corrections.

eta_thermal.label: Thermal Efficiency
eta_thermal.description: Process efficiency metric for Lean Six Sigma.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Moisture Impact on Conductivity
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Percentage increase in conductivity due to moisture.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Aging Impact on Conductivity
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Percentage change due to aging factor.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Temperature Nonlinearity Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Deviation from linear Fourier behavior due to temperature-dependent properties.

suggestedActions.action: Implement moisture barrier or drying process to reduce moisture content below 5%.
Consider material replacement or rejuvenation treatment to restore thermal performance.
Increase material thickness by 20% to meet minimum R-value standards (ISO 6946).
Recalibrate heat flux sensors and thermocouples. Perform Gage R&R study per Six Sigma.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Conductivity
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Effective conductivity adjusted for measurement confidence. Low confidence applies ±15% uncertainty, medium ±5%, high ±1%.
